text_original: Motse said: If the rulers of to-day sincerely wish to b© careful in condemnation and commendation, judicious in rewards and punishments, and temperate in government and jurisdiction;.
text_normalized: Motse said: If the rulers of to-day sincerely wish to b© careful in condemnation and commendation, judicious in rewards and punishments, and temperate in government and jurisdiction;.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: There is an ancient saying that, | when one is not successful in making out plans then predict the future by the past and learn about the absent from what is present.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: There is an ancient saying that, | when one is not successful in making out plans then predict the future by the past and learn about the absent from what is present.

text_original: When one plans like this then one can be intelligent.
text_normalized: When one plans like this then one can be intelligent.

text_original: Now, about a country going to war.
text_normalized: Now, about a country going to war.

text_original: If it is in winter it will be too cold; if it is in summer it will be too hot.
text_normalized: If it is in winter it will be too cold; if it is in summer it will be too hot.

text_original: So it should be neither in winter nor in summer.
text_normalized: So it should be neither in winter nor in summer.

text_original: If it is in spring it will take people away from sowing and planting; if it is in autumn it will take people away from reaping and harvesting.
text_normalized: If it is in spring it will take people away from sowing and planting; if it is in autumn it will take people away from reaping and harvesting.

text_original: Should they be taken away in either of these seasons, innumerable people would die of hxmger and cold.
text_normalized: Should they be taken away in either of these seasons, innumerable people would die of hxmger and cold.

text_original: And, when the army sets out, the bamboo arrows, the feather flags, the house tents, the armour, the shields, the sword hilts — innumerable quantities of these will break and rot and never come back.
text_normalized: And, when the army sets out, the bamboo arrows, the feather flags, the house tents, the armour, the shields, the sword hilts — innumerable quantities of these will break and rot and never come back.

text_original: The spears, the lances, the swords, the poniards, the chariots, the carts — innimierable quantities of these will break and rot and never come back.
text_normalized: The spears, the lances, the swords, the poniards, the chariots, the carts — innimierable quantities of these will break and rot and never come back.

text_original: Then innumerable horses and oxen will start out fat and come back lean or will not return at all And innumerable people will die because their food will be cut off and cannot be supplied on account of the great distances of the roads.
text_normalized: Then innumerable horses and oxen will start out fat and come back lean or will not return at all And innumerable people will die because their food will be cut off and cannot be supplied on account of the great distances of the roads.

text_original: And innumerable people will be sick and die of the constant danger and the irregularity of eating and drinking and the extremes of hunger and over-eating.
text_normalized: And innumerable people will be sick and die of the constant danger and the irregularity of eating and drinking and the extremes of hunger and over-eating.

text_original: Then, the army will be lost in large numbers or enthely; in either case the number will be innumerable.
text_normalized: Then, the army will be lost in large numbers or enthely; in either case the number will be innumerable.

text_original: And this means the spirits will lose their worshippers, and the number of these will also be innumerable.
text_normalized: And this means the spirits will lose their worshippers, and the number of these will also be innumerable.

text_original: Why then does the government deprive the people of their opportunities and benefits to such a great extent?
text_normalized: Why then does the government deprive the people of their opportunities and benefits to such a great extent?

text_original: It has been answered: '‘I covet the fame of the victor and the possessions obtainable through the conquest.
text_normalized: It has been answered: '‘I covet the fame of the victor and the possessions obtainable through the conquest.

text_original: So I doit." Motse said: But when we consider the victory as such, there is nothing useful about it.
text_normalized: So I doit." Motse said: But when we consider the victory as such, there is nothing useful about it.

text_original: When we consider the possessions obtained through it, it does not even make up for the loss.
text_normalized: When we consider the possessions obtained through it, it does not even make up for the loss.

text_original: Now about the siege of a city of three li or a kuo of seven li — if these could be obtained without the use of weapons or the killing of lives, it would be all right.
text_normalized: Now about the siege of a city of three li or a kuo of seven li — if these could be obtained without the use of weapons or the killing of lives, it would be all right.

text_original: But (as a matter of fact) those killed must be counted by the ten thousand, those widowed or left solitary must be counted by the thousand, before a city of three li or a kuo of seven li All Chinese cities until very recently were walled in for protection.
text_normalized: But (as a matter of fact) those killed must be counted by the ten thousand, those widowed or left solitary must be counted by the thousand, before a city of three li or a kuo of seven li All Chinese cities until very recently were walled in for protection.

text_original: The inner wall is called ch’eng m, city, or city wall, and the onter wall is called " kuo conld be captured.
text_normalized: The inner wall is called ch’eng m, city, or city wall, and the onter wall is called " kuo conld be captured.

text_original: Moreover the states of ten thousand chariots now have empty towns to be counted by the thousand j which can be entered without conquest; and their extensive lands to be counted by the ten thousand (of mu), which can be cultivated without conquest.
text_normalized: Moreover the states of ten thousand chariots now have empty towns to be counted by the thousand j which can be entered without conquest; and their extensive lands to be counted by the ten thousand (of mu), which can be cultivated without conquest.

text_original: So, land is abundant but people are few.
text_normalized: So, land is abundant but people are few.

text_original: Now to pursue the people to death and aggravate the danger feared by both superiors and subordinates in order to obtain an empty city — this is to give up what is needed and to treasure what is already in abundance.
text_normalized: Now to pursue the people to death and aggravate the danger feared by both superiors and subordinates in order to obtain an empty city — this is to give up what is needed and to treasure what is already in abundance.

text_original: Such an undertaking is not in accordance with the interest of the country.
text_normalized: Such an undertaking is not in accordance with the interest of the country.

text_original: Those who endeavour to gloss over offensive wars would say: "In the south there are the lords of Ching and Yiieh, and in the north there are the lords of Chh and Chin.
text_normalized: Those who endeavour to gloss over offensive wars would say: "In the south there are the lords of Ching and Yiieh, and in the north there are the lords of Chh and Chin.

text_original: When their states were first assigned to them, they were but a hundred li square in area, and but a few tens of thousands in number of people.
text_normalized: When their states were first assigned to them, they were but a hundred li square in area, and but a few tens of thousands in number of people.

text_original: By means of wars and attacks, their areas have increased to several thousand li square and the people to several million.
text_normalized: By means of wars and attacks, their areas have increased to several thousand li square and the people to several million.

text_original: So, then, offensive wars are not to be condemned." Motse said: The four or five states may have reaped their benefits, still it is not conduct according to the Tao.
text_normalized: So, then, offensive wars are not to be condemned." Motse said: The four or five states may have reaped their benefits, still it is not conduct according to the Tao.

text_original: It is like the physician giving his drugs to the patients.
text_normalized: It is like the physician giving his drugs to the patients.

text_original: If a physician should give all the sick in the world a uniform drug among the ten thousand who took it there might be four or five who were benefited, still it is not to be said to be a common (commonly beneficial) medicine.
text_normalized: If a physician should give all the sick in the world a uniform drug among the ten thousand who took it there might be four or five who were benefited, still it is not to be said to be a common (commonly beneficial) medicine.

text_original: Thus a filial son will not give it to his parent and a loyal minister will not give it to his king.
text_normalized: Thus a filial son will not give it to his parent and a loyal minister will not give it to his king.

text_original: After the empire was in the ancient time divided into states a great many of them died of attacks — the earlier cases we hear of through the ear, the recent cases we saw by the eye.
text_normalized: After the empire was in the ancient time divided into states a great many of them died of attacks — the earlier cases we hear of through the ear, the recent cases we saw by the eye.

text_original: How do we know it is so?
text_normalized: How do we know it is so?

text_original: In the east there was the state of Chii.
text_normalized: In the east there was the state of Chii.

text_original: It was a small state situated in the midst of big states.
text_normalized: It was a small state situated in the midst of big states.

text_original: It did not show’ respect and obedience to the big states, and the latter therefore did not like it or favour it.
text_normalized: It did not show’ respect and obedience to the big states, and the latter therefore did not like it or favour it.

text_original: So, on the east Ytieh cut and appropriated its land by force, and from the w’est Ch'i swallowed it up altogether.
text_normalized: So, on the east Ytieh cut and appropriated its land by force, and from the w’est Ch'i swallowed it up altogether.

text_original: And it was due to offensive wars that Chii died betw'een two big states.
text_normalized: And it was due to offensive wars that Chii died betw'een two big states.

text_original: And it was due to offensive war too that in the south Ch’en and Ts'ai were extinguished by Wu and Yiieh.
text_normalized: And it was due to offensive war too that in the south Ch’en and Ts'ai were extinguished by Wu and Yiieh.

text_original: And it was also due to offensive wars that in the north Pii T’u Ho perished among Yen, Tai, Hu and Mo.
text_normalized: And it was also due to offensive wars that in the north Pii T’u Ho perished among Yen, Tai, Hu and Mo.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: If the rulers now really desire gain and avert loss, desire security and avert danger, they cannot but condemn offensive wars.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: If the rulers now really desire gain and avert loss, desire security and avert danger, they cannot but condemn offensive wars.

text_original: Those who endeavour to gloss over offensive wars would say: These states perished because they could not gather and employ their multitudes.
text_normalized: Those who endeavour to gloss over offensive wars would say: These states perished because they could not gather and employ their multitudes.

text_original: I can gather and employ my multitudes and wage war with them; who, then, dare to be unsubmissive?
text_normalized: I can gather and employ my multitudes and wage war with them; who, then, dare to be unsubmissive?

text_original: " Motse said: You might be able to gather and employ your multitudes, but can you compare yourself with the ancient Ho Lii of Wu?
text_normalized: " Motse said: You might be able to gather and employ your multitudes, but can you compare yourself with the ancient Ho Lii of Wu?

text_original: Ho Lii of Wu (about 610 b.c.) in the ancient days drilled his soldiers seven years.
text_normalized: Ho Lii of Wu (about 610 b.c.) in the ancient days drilled his soldiers seven years.

text_original: With armour on and weapons in hand they could cover three hundred li (in a day) before encamping (for the night).
text_normalized: With armour on and weapons in hand they could cover three hundred li (in a day) before encamping (for the night).

text_original: Passing Chu Lin, they emerged at the narrow Pass of Min.
text_normalized: Passing Chu Lin, they emerged at the narrow Pass of Min.

text_original: They engaged in battle (with the state of Ch’u) at Po Chii.
text_normalized: They engaged in battle (with the state of Ch’u) at Po Chii.

text_original: Subduing Ch’u, (Ho Lii) gave audience to Sung and Lu.
text_normalized: Subduing Ch’u, (Ho Lii) gave audience to Sung and Lu.

text_original: By the time of Fu Ch'ai, he attacked ChT in the north, encamped on the Wen River, fought at Ai Ling and greatly defeated Ch'i and compelled surety from them at Mt.
text_normalized: By the time of Fu Ch'ai, he attacked ChT in the north, encamped on the Wen River, fought at Ai Ling and greatly defeated Ch'i and compelled surety from them at Mt.

text_original: In the east he attacked Yiieh, crossing the Three Rivers and the Five Lakes, and compelled surety at Kuei Chi.
text_normalized: In the east he attacked Yiieh, crossing the Three Rivers and the Five Lakes, and compelled surety at Kuei Chi.

text_original: None of the nine tribes dared to show disrespect.
text_normalized: None of the nine tribes dared to show disrespect.

text_original: Reaching home, however, he would not reward the orphaned or give to the numerous rustics.
text_normalized: Reaching home, however, he would not reward the orphaned or give to the numerous rustics.

text_original: He depended on his own might, gloated over his success, praised his own cleverness, and neglected instructing and training his people.
text_normalized: He depended on his own might, gloated over his success, praised his own cleverness, and neglected instructing and training his people.

text_original: He built the Monument of Ku Su which was not completed even in seven years.
text_normalized: He built the Monument of Ku Su which was not completed even in seven years.

text_original: By this time (the people of Wu) felt tired and disheartened.
text_normalized: By this time (the people of Wu) felt tired and disheartened.

text_original: Seeing the friction between the superior and the subordinates in Wu, Kou Chien of Ytieh gathered his multitudes to take revenge.
text_normalized: Seeing the friction between the superior and the subordinates in Wu, Kou Chien of Ytieh gathered his multitudes to take revenge.

text_original: He broke into its kuo on the north, moved away its royal boat, and surrounded its palace.
text_normalized: He broke into its kuo on the north, moved away its royal boat, and surrounded its palace.

text_original: And thus Wu perished.
text_normalized: And thus Wu perished.

text_original: Some time ago Chin had six ministers and Chih Po (about spread of Iiis courageous name tluoiigli war and battle.
text_normalized: Some time ago Chin had six ministers and Chih Po (about spread of Iiis courageous name tluoiigli war and battle.

text_original: So lie ranked his brave warriors and arranged his boat and chariot forces.
text_normalized: So lie ranked his brave warriors and arranged his boat and chariot forces.

text_original: He attacked (the house of) Chung Hsing and seized it.
text_normalized: He attacked (the house of) Chung Hsing and seized it.

text_original: This showed to him that his plans were satisfactory.
text_normalized: This showed to him that his plans were satisfactory.

text_original: Then he attacked Pan and totally defeated him.
text_normalized: Then he attacked Pan and totally defeated him.

text_original: Thus he absorbed three families into one.
text_normalized: Thus he absorbed three families into one.

text_original: He did not stop even there, but surrounded Minister Hsiang of Chao at Chin Yang.
text_normalized: He did not stop even there, but surrounded Minister Hsiang of Chao at Chin Yang.

text_original: By this time Han and Wei came together and deliberated, saying: An ancient proverb says: ‘ When the lips are removed the teeth will become cold.’ The house of Chao dying in the morning we would be following it in the evening: the house of Chao dying in the evening we would be following it in the morning.
text_normalized: By this time Han and Wei came together and deliberated, saying: An ancient proverb says: ‘ When the lips are removed the teeth will become cold.’ The house of Chao dying in the morning we would be following it in the evening: the house of Chao dying in the evening we would be following it in the morning.

text_original: A poet sings: ‘ If the fish would not act while yet in water, what can it do when it is already placed on land?
text_normalized: A poet sings: ‘ If the fish would not act while yet in water, what can it do when it is already placed on land?

text_original: ’ " Thereupon the three ministers worked with united strength and a single mind, opening passes and blazing trails, putting on armour and arousing the warriors.
text_normalized: ’ " Thereupon the three ministers worked with united strength and a single mind, opening passes and blazing trails, putting on armour and arousing the warriors.

text_original: With Han and Wei from without and Chao from within, they battled Chih Po and totally defeated him.
text_normalized: With Han and Wei from without and Chao from within, they battled Chih Po and totally defeated him.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: An ancient proverb says: " The superior man would not go to water hut to man for a mirror.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: An ancient proverb says: " The superior man would not go to water hut to man for a mirror.

text_original: In water as a mirror one sees only one’s face; in man as a mirror one can predict good and bad luck." Have those who now regard offensive wars as beneficial made use of Chih Po’s story?
text_normalized: In water as a mirror one sees only one’s face; in man as a mirror one can predict good and bad luck." Have those who now regard offensive wars as beneficial made use of Chih Po’s story?

text_original: It is plainly discernible to be not auspicious but ominous.
text_normalized: It is plainly discernible to be not auspicious but ominous.

text_original: to the spirits in the middle sphere, and to the people below?
text_normalized: to the spirits in the middle sphere, and to the people below?

text_original: Certainly no other reason is needed for praise than to be useful to Heaven on high, to the spirits in the middle, and to the people below.
text_normalized: Certainly no other reason is needed for praise than to be useful to Heaven on high, to the spirits in the middle, and to the people below.

text_original: Even the stupid would say it is praise-worthy when it is helpful to Heaven on high, to the spirits in the middle, and to the people below.
text_normalized: Even the stupid would say it is praise-worthy when it is helpful to Heaven on high, to the spirits in the middle, and to the people below.

text_original: And what the world agrees on is just the way of the sage-kings.
text_normalized: And what the world agrees on is just the way of the sage-kings.

text_original: Now the feudal lords in the empire still attack and assault each other.
text_normalized: Now the feudal lords in the empire still attack and assault each other.

text_original: This is to praise the principle without under-standing its real meaning.
text_normalized: This is to praise the principle without under-standing its real meaning.

text_original: They resemble the blind man who uses with others the names of black and white but cannot discriminate between such objects.
text_normalized: They resemble the blind man who uses with others the names of black and white but cannot discriminate between such objects.

text_original: Can this be said to be real discrimination?
text_normalized: Can this be said to be real discrimination?

text_original: Therefore, deliberating for the empire, the ancient wise men always considered the real (meaning of the) principles, and acted accordingly.
text_normalized: Therefore, deliberating for the empire, the ancient wise men always considered the real (meaning of the) principles, and acted accordingly.

text_original: Hence they were no more uncertain in their actions.
text_normalized: Hence they were no more uncertain in their actions.

text_original: All desires, far or near, were satisfied, and Heaven, the spirits, and the people were all blessed.
text_normalized: All desires, far or near, were satisfied, and Heaven, the spirits, and the people were all blessed.

text_original: Such was the way of the wise men.
text_normalized: Such was the way of the wise men.

text_original: Governing the empire, the ancient magnanimous men always worked for mutual satisfaction with the large states, brought the empire into harmony, and centralized all that was within the four seas.
text_normalized: Governing the empire, the ancient magnanimous men always worked for mutual satisfaction with the large states, brought the empire into harmony, and centralized all that was within the four seas.

text_original: Then they led the people in the empire diligently to do service to God, hills and rivers, and the spirits and ghosts.
text_normalized: Then they led the people in the empire diligently to do service to God, hills and rivers, and the spirits and ghosts.

text_original: Many were the benefits to the people and great was their success.
text_normalized: Many were the benefits to the people and great was their success.

text_original: Thereupon Heaven rewarded them, the spirits enriched them, and the people praised them.
text_normalized: Thereupon Heaven rewarded them, the spirits enriched them, and the people praised them.

text_original: The high honour of Son of Heaven was conferred upon them and the great wealth of the whole world was given to them.
text_normalized: The high honour of Son of Heaven was conferred upon them and the great wealth of the whole world was given to them.

text_original: Their names partake of (the permanence and greatness of) Heaven and Earth and are not forgotten even to this day.
text_normalized: Their names partake of (the permanence and greatness of) Heaven and Earth and are not forgotten even to this day.

text_original: Such is the way of the wise and such is the way by which the ancient kings came to possess the world.
text_normalized: Such is the way of the wise and such is the way by which the ancient kings came to possess the world.

text_original: The rulers and lords of to-day are quite different.
text_normalized: The rulers and lords of to-day are quite different.

text_original: They all rank their warriors and arrange their boat and chariot forces; they make their armour strong and weapons sharp in order to attack some innocent state.
text_normalized: They all rank their warriors and arrange their boat and chariot forces; they make their armour strong and weapons sharp in order to attack some innocent state.

text_original: Entering the state they cut down the grain fields and fell the trees and woods; they tear down the inner and outer walls of the city and fill up the ditches and ponds; they seize and Idll the sacrificial animals and burn down the ancestral temple; they kill and murder the people and exterminate the aged and weak; they move away the treasures and valuables.
text_normalized: Entering the state they cut down the grain fields and fell the trees and woods; they tear down the inner and outer walls of the city and fill up the ditches and ponds; they seize and Idll the sacrificial animals and burn down the ancestral temple; they kill and murder the people and exterminate the aged and weak; they move away the treasures and valuables.

text_original: The soldiers are encouraged to advance by being told: To suffer death is the highest (service you can render), to kill many is the next, to be wounded is the lowest.
text_normalized: The soldiers are encouraged to advance by being told: To suffer death is the highest (service you can render), to kill many is the next, to be wounded is the lowest.

text_original: But if you should drop out from your rank and attempt to sneak away, the penalty will be death without moderation.’' Thus the soldiers are put to fear.
text_normalized: But if you should drop out from your rank and attempt to sneak away, the penalty will be death without moderation.’' Thus the soldiers are put to fear.

text_original: Now to capture a state and to destroy an army, to disturb and torture the people, and to set at naught the aspirations of the sages by confusion— is this intended to bless Heaven?
text_normalized: Now to capture a state and to destroy an army, to disturb and torture the people, and to set at naught the aspirations of the sages by confusion— is this intended to bless Heaven?

text_original: But the people of Heaven are gathered together to besiege the towns belonging to Heaven.
text_normalized: But the people of Heaven are gathered together to besiege the towns belonging to Heaven.

text_original: This is to murder men of Heaven and dispossess the spirits of their altars and to ruin the state and to kill the sacrificial animals.
text_normalized: This is to murder men of Heaven and dispossess the spirits of their altars and to ruin the state and to kill the sacrificial animals.

text_original: It is then not a blessing to Heaven on high.
text_normalized: It is then not a blessing to Heaven on high.

text_original: Is it intended to bless the spirits?
text_normalized: Is it intended to bless the spirits?

text_original: But men of Heaven are murdered, spirits are deprived of their sacrifices, the earlier kings are neglected, the multitude are tortured and the people are scattered; it is then not a blessing to the spirits in the middle.
text_normalized: But men of Heaven are murdered, spirits are deprived of their sacrifices, the earlier kings are neglected, the multitude are tortured and the people are scattered; it is then not a blessing to the spirits in the middle.

text_original: Is it intended to bless the people?
text_normalized: Is it intended to bless the people?

text_original: But the blessing of the people by killing them off must be very meagre.
text_normalized: But the blessing of the people by killing them off must be very meagre.

text_original: And when we calculate the expense, which is the root of the calamities to living, we find the property of innumerable people is exhausted.
text_normalized: And when we calculate the expense, which is the root of the calamities to living, we find the property of innumerable people is exhausted.

text_original: It is, then, not a blessing to the people below either.
text_normalized: It is, then, not a blessing to the people below either.

text_original: Now that the armies are intended for mutual destruction, it is evident: If the general be not courageous, if soldiers be not brave, if weapons be not sharp, if drills be not frequent, if the force be not large, if generals be not harmonious, if power be not august, if a siege be not enduring, if an assault be not speedy, if people be not strongly bound together, if determination be not firm — if this be so, the (other) feudal lords will suspect.
text_normalized: Now that the armies are intended for mutual destruction, it is evident: If the general be not courageous, if soldiers be not brave, if weapons be not sharp, if drills be not frequent, if the force be not large, if generals be not harmonious, if power be not august, if a siege be not enduring, if an assault be not speedy, if people be not strongly bound together, if determination be not firm — if this be so, the (other) feudal lords will suspect.

text_original: When feudal lords entertain suspicion, enemies will be stirred up and cause anxiety, and the morale will be weakened.
text_normalized: When feudal lords entertain suspicion, enemies will be stirred up and cause anxiety, and the morale will be weakened.

text_original: On the other hand, if every preparation is in good shape and the state goes out to engage in war, then the state will lose its men and the people will neglect their vocations.
text_normalized: On the other hand, if every preparation is in good shape and the state goes out to engage in war, then the state will lose its men and the people will neglect their vocations.

text_original: Have we not heard it said that, when a warring state goes on an expedition, of the officers there no must be several hundred, of the common people there must be several thousand, and of the soldiers and prisoners there must be ten thousand, before the army can set out?
text_normalized: Have we not heard it said that, when a warring state goes on an expedition, of the officers there no must be several hundred, of the common people there must be several thousand, and of the soldiers and prisoners there must be ten thousand, before the army can set out?

text_original: It may last for several years, or, at the shortest, several months.
text_normalized: It may last for several years, or, at the shortest, several months.

text_original: So, the superior will have no time to attend to government, the officials will have no time to attend to their offices, the farmers will have no time to sow or reap, the w-omen will have no time to weave or spin: that is, the state will lose its men and the people will neglect their vocations.
text_normalized: So, the superior will have no time to attend to government, the officials will have no time to attend to their offices, the farmers will have no time to sow or reap, the w-omen will have no time to weave or spin: that is, the state will lose its men and the people will neglect their vocations.

text_original: Besides, the chariots will break and horses will be exhausted.
text_normalized: Besides, the chariots will break and horses will be exhausted.

text_original: As to tents, army supplies, and soldiers’ equipment — if one-fifth of these can remain (after the war) it would already be beyond expectation.
text_normalized: As to tents, army supplies, and soldiers’ equipment — if one-fifth of these can remain (after the war) it would already be beyond expectation.

text_original: Moreover, innumerable men will be missing and lost on the way, and will become sick from the long distances, meagre rations, hunger and cold, and die in the ditches.
text_normalized: Moreover, innumerable men will be missing and lost on the way, and will become sick from the long distances, meagre rations, hunger and cold, and die in the ditches.

text_original: Now the calamity to the people and the world is tremendous.
text_normalized: Now the calamity to the people and the world is tremendous.

text_original: Yet the rulers enjoy doing it.
text_normalized: Yet the rulers enjoy doing it.

text_original: This means they enjoy injuring and exterminating the people; is this not perversity?
text_normalized: This means they enjoy injuring and exterminating the people; is this not perversity?

text_original: The most warring states in the empire tO"day are Ch’i, Chin, Ch’u, and Yxieh.
text_normalized: The most warring states in the empire tO"day are Ch’i, Chin, Ch’u, and Yxieh.

text_original: These four states are all successful in the world.
text_normalized: These four states are all successful in the world.

text_original: Even if their people be increased tenfold, still they could not consume all that their land could produce.
text_normalized: Even if their people be increased tenfold, still they could not consume all that their land could produce.

text_original: That is, they are in need of men while they have a surplus of land.
text_normalized: That is, they are in need of men while they have a surplus of land.

text_original: Still they strove against each other to possess more land.
text_normalized: Still they strove against each other to possess more land.

text_original: This is to neglect what is needed and to value what is already in plenty.
text_normalized: This is to neglect what is needed and to value what is already in plenty.

text_original: The warring lords would gloss over (their conduct) with arguments to confute Motse, saying: ‘‘Do you condemn attack and assault as unrighteous and not beneficial?
text_normalized: The warring lords would gloss over (their conduct) with arguments to confute Motse, saying: ‘‘Do you condemn attack and assault as unrighteous and not beneficial?

text_original: But, anciently, Yii made war on the Prince of Miao, T’ang on Chieh, and King Wu on Chow.
text_normalized: But, anciently, Yii made war on the Prince of Miao, T’ang on Chieh, and King Wu on Chow.

text_original: Yet these are regarded as sages.
text_normalized: Yet these are regarded as sages.

text_original: What is your explanation for this?
text_normalized: What is your explanation for this?

text_original: Motse said: You have not examined the terminology of my teaching and you do not understand its motive.
text_normalized: Motse said: You have not examined the terminology of my teaching and you do not understand its motive.

text_original: What they did is not to be called " attack " but punishment Anciently, the three Miao tribes were in great confusion.
text_normalized: What they did is not to be called " attack " but punishment Anciently, the three Miao tribes were in great confusion.

text_original: Heaven ordered their destruction.
text_normalized: Heaven ordered their destruction.

text_original: The sun rose at night.
text_normalized: The sun rose at night.

text_original: It rained blood for three days.
text_normalized: It rained blood for three days.

text_original: Dragons emerged in the temple and dogs cried in the market place.
text_normalized: Dragons emerged in the temple and dogs cried in the market place.

text_original: Ice came in summer and earth cracked until water gushed forth.
text_normalized: Ice came in summer and earth cracked until water gushed forth.

text_original: The five grains appeared in mutation.
text_normalized: The five grains appeared in mutation.

text_original: At these, the people were greatly shocked.
text_normalized: At these, the people were greatly shocked.

text_original: Kao Yang> then gave command (to Yii) in the Yuan Palace.
text_normalized: Kao Yang> then gave command (to Yii) in the Yuan Palace.

text_original: Yii held the imperial jade order in hand and set forth to conquer Miao.
text_normalized: Yii held the imperial jade order in hand and set forth to conquer Miao.

text_original: Amidst thunder and lightning, a god with the face of a man and the body of a bird was revealed to be waiting upon (Yii) with the kuei in hand.
text_normalized: Amidst thunder and lightning, a god with the face of a man and the body of a bird was revealed to be waiting upon (Yii) with the kuei in hand.

text_original: The general of Miao was brought down by an arrow, and the Miao army was set in great confusion.
text_normalized: The general of Miao was brought down by an arrow, and the Miao army was set in great confusion.

text_original: And the Miao tribes became less and less significant ever after.
text_normalized: And the Miao tribes became less and less significant ever after.

text_original: Having conquered Miao, Yii set apart the hills and rivers (by names), and ordered things into high and low.
text_normalized: Having conquered Miao, Yii set apart the hills and rivers (by names), and ordered things into high and low.

text_original: With sacrifices he cannot be made out in this connexion.
text_normalized: With sacrifices he cannot be made out in this connexion.

text_original: This seems to be a good guess.
text_normalized: This seems to be a good guess.

text_original: set up the four bordering countries, and neither spirits nor men revolted (any more).
text_normalized: set up the four bordering countries, and neither spirits nor men revolted (any more).

text_original: So there was peace in the world.
text_normalized: So there was peace in the world.

text_original: This was the reason why Yii made war on the Miao.
text_normalized: This was the reason why Yii made war on the Miao.

text_original: When it came to King Chieh of Hsia, Heaven gave severe order.
text_normalized: When it came to King Chieh of Hsia, Heaven gave severe order.

text_original: Sun and moon did not appear on time.
text_normalized: Sun and moon did not appear on time.

text_original: Winter and summer came irregularly.
text_normalized: Winter and summer came irregularly.

text_original: The five grains were dried up to death.
text_normalized: The five grains were dried up to death.

text_original: Ghosts called in the country, and cranes shrieked for more than ten nights.
text_normalized: Ghosts called in the country, and cranes shrieked for more than ten nights.

text_original: Heaven then commissioned T’ang in the Piao Palace, to receive the great trust that had been given to Hsia, as the conduct of Hsia fell into great perversity.
text_normalized: Heaven then commissioned T’ang in the Piao Palace, to receive the great trust that had been given to Hsia, as the conduct of Hsia fell into great perversity.

text_original: Only then dared T ang to lead his multitude and enter the borders of Hsia.
text_normalized: Only then dared T ang to lead his multitude and enter the borders of Hsia.

text_original: And he let the deserters of the enemy destroy the cities of Hsia.
text_normalized: And he let the deserters of the enemy destroy the cities of Hsia.

text_original: Soon after, a god came and told him; ‘‘ The conduct of Hsia is in great con-fusion.
text_normalized: Soon after, a god came and told him; ‘‘ The conduct of Hsia is in great con-fusion.

text_original: Go and punish him.
text_normalized: Go and punish him.

text_original: I will surely let you destroy him, as I have my orders from Heaven." Heaven ordered Chu Yung to send down fire on the north-western corner of the city of Hsia.
text_normalized: I will surely let you destroy him, as I have my orders from Heaven." Heaven ordered Chu Yung to send down fire on the north-western corner of the city of Hsia.

text_original: Thus T'ang led the men of Chieh and conquered Hsia.
text_normalized: Thus T'ang led the men of Chieh and conquered Hsia.

text_original: He then gave audience to the feudal lords at Po.
text_normalized: He then gave audience to the feudal lords at Po.

text_original: He revealed and made known the will of Heaven and spread it in the four directions, and none of the feudal lords in the empire dared to show disrespect.
text_normalized: He revealed and made known the will of Heaven and spread it in the four directions, and none of the feudal lords in the empire dared to show disrespect.

text_original: This was the reason why T'ang punished Chieh.
text_normalized: This was the reason why T'ang punished Chieh.

text_original: When it came to the regime of King Chow of Shang, his conduct was not acceptable to Heaven.
text_normalized: When it came to the regime of King Chow of Shang, his conduct was not acceptable to Heaven.

text_original: Sacrifices were not according to seasons.
text_normalized: Sacrifices were not according to seasons.

text_original: Even in the night.
text_normalized: Even in the night.

text_original: It rained sand for ten days at Po.
text_normalized: It rained sand for ten days at Po.

text_original: The nine caldrons moved from their place.
text_normalized: The nine caldrons moved from their place.

text_original: Witches appeared in the dark and ghosts sighed at night.
text_normalized: Witches appeared in the dark and ghosts sighed at night.

text_original: Some women turned into men.
text_normalized: Some women turned into men.

text_original: Flesh came down from Heaven like rain.
text_normalized: Flesh came down from Heaven like rain.

text_original: Thorny brambles covered up the national highways.
text_normalized: Thorny brambles covered up the national highways.

text_original: Yet the king became even more dissolute.
text_normalized: Yet the king became even more dissolute.

text_original: A red bird holding a kuei by its beak alighted on Mt.

text_normalized: Yi K’uei started at Yu Gbii, and became lord of Yiieb.

text_normalized: Unc T’ang and Lii Shang were first appointed to rule over the states of Cbin (1107 b.c.) and of Cb’i (about 1120 B.c.) respectively.

text_original: All these started with a few hundred li square of land.
text_normalized: All these started with a few hundred li square of land.

text_original: On account of their capture of other states, now each of them has a quarter of the empire.
text_normalized: On account of their capture of other states, now each of them has a quarter of the empire.

text_original: What is your explana-tion for this V Motse said: You have not examined the terminology of my teachings and you do not understand the underlying principle.
text_normalized: What is your explana-tion for this V Motse said: You have not examined the terminology of my teachings and you do not understand the underlying principle.

text_original: In ancient times the emperor commissioned feudal lords numbering more than ten thousand.
text_normalized: In ancient times the emperor commissioned feudal lords numbering more than ten thousand.

text_original: On account of absorption of one state by another, all of the more than ten thousand states have disappeared with only the four remaining.
text_normalized: On account of absorption of one state by another, all of the more than ten thousand states have disappeared with only the four remaining.

text_original: This is like the physician who attends more than ten thousand patients but cures only four.
text_normalized: This is like the physician who attends more than ten thousand patients but cures only four.

text_original: Such an one is not to be said to be a good physician.
text_normalized: Such an one is not to be said to be a good physician.

text_original: The warring lords would again gloss over (their conduct) with arguments, saying: (I wage war) not because I am still discontented with my gold and jade, my children and my land.
text_normalized: The warring lords would again gloss over (their conduct) with arguments, saying: (I wage war) not because I am still discontented with my gold and jade, my children and my land.

text_original: I want to have my name as a righteous ruler established in the world and draw the other feudal lords to me with my virtue." Motse said: If there were some one who would establish his righteous name for justice in the world and draw the feudal lords to him with his virtue, the submission of the whole world to him could be awaited while standing.
text_normalized: I want to have my name as a righteous ruler established in the world and draw the other feudal lords to me with my virtue." Motse said: If there were some one who would establish his righteous name for justice in the world and draw the feudal lords to him with his virtue, the submission of the whole world to him could be awaited while standing.

text_original: For the world has long been in turmoil and war, and it is weary like the boy at playing horse.
text_normalized: For the world has long been in turmoil and war, and it is weary like the boy at playing horse.

text_original: If only there were some one who would first benefit the other feudal lords in mutual good faith!
text_normalized: If only there were some one who would first benefit the other feudal lords in mutual good faith!

text_original: When some large state acts unrighteously, he would join in the sorrow; when some large state attacks some smaller one he would join in the rescue; when the outer and the inner walls of the city of the small state are in ruin he would demand their repair; when cloth and grains are exhausted he would supply them; when money and silk are insuiBSicient he would share his own — to befriend the large state in this way, the large state will be pleased, to befriend the small state in this way, the small state will be pleased.
text_normalized: When some large state acts unrighteously, he would join in the sorrow; when some large state attacks some smaller one he would join in the rescue; when the outer and the inner walls of the city of the small state are in ruin he would demand their repair; when cloth and grains are exhausted he would supply them; when money and silk are insuiBSicient he would share his own — to befriend the large state in this way, the large state will be pleased, to befriend the small state in this way, the small state will be pleased.

text_original: With the others tired out and one’s self at ease, one’s armour and weapon would be stronger.
text_normalized: With the others tired out and one’s self at ease, one’s armour and weapon would be stronger.

text_original: When with kindness and mercy we help the people in their need, the people will be drawn over.
text_normalized: When with kindness and mercy we help the people in their need, the people will be drawn over.

text_original: When good government is substituted for aggressive war, the country will be benefited many fold.
text_normalized: When good government is substituted for aggressive war, the country will be benefited many fold.

text_original: When the expenses of the army is limited and the evils of the feudal lords removed, then we shall enjoy prosperity.
text_normalized: When the expenses of the army is limited and the evils of the feudal lords removed, then we shall enjoy prosperity.

text_original: Let the control (of the army) be judicious and the cause be righteous.
text_normalized: Let the control (of the army) be judicious and the cause be righteous.

text_original: Be lenient to the people and place confidence in the army — with this to meet the forces of the feudal lords, there can be no equal in the world.
text_normalized: Be lenient to the people and place confidence in the army — with this to meet the forces of the feudal lords, there can be no equal in the world.

text_original: And the benefits to the world will be innumerable.
text_normalized: And the benefits to the world will be innumerable.

text_original: This is what will benefit the world, yet the rulers do not understand making use of it: such may be said to be ignorance of the great thing in the world.
text_normalized: This is what will benefit the world, yet the rulers do not understand making use of it: such may be said to be ignorance of the great thing in the world.

text_original: of the world sincerely desire to procure benefits and avert calamities for the world-if they desire to do righteousness and be superior men, if they desire to strike the way of the sage-kings on the one hand and bless the people on the other ■—if so, the doctrine of Condemnation of Offensive War should not be left unheeded.
text_normalized: of the world sincerely desire to procure benefits and avert calamities for the world-if they desire to do righteousness and be superior men, if they desire to strike the way of the sage-kings on the one hand and bless the people on the other ■—if so, the doctrine of Condemnation of Offensive War should not be left unheeded.
